result.summary: The page has two notable visual defects: the header CTA button appears empty with no label, and the 'SCROLL DOWN' element overlaps the legal terms text, making the fine print unreadable. A few minor accessibility/readability concerns also exist.

issues.description: The yellow button in the top-right header is completely blank — it contains no text or icon, so users cannot tell it is likely the 'Sign Up' or 'Log In' action.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the button label renders (e.g., 'Join Now' / 'Log In'). Check for a failed font/asset load or a missing text node in the component.

issues.description: The 'SCROLL DOWN' indicator overlaps the terms & conditions fine-print paragraph, obscuring several lines of legally required disclosure text.
issues.suggestion: Reposition the scroll indicator below the T&C block or add sufficient padding/margin so the legal copy remains fully visible and unobstructed.

issues.description: The terms & conditions text at the bottom (blue text on yellow background, very small size) is extremely hard to read even where it isn't overlapped.
issues.suggestion: Increase font size and use a higher-contrast color combination (e.g., dark navy on light background) to meet WCAG contrast guidelines for legal copy.

issues.description: The overlapping SCROLL DOWN box has a white background card that appears cut into the yellow section awkwardly, creating a visually broken 'patch' effect over the disclaimer.
issues.suggestion: Give the scroll indicator its own dedicated section or make its background transparent so it doesn't look like a misplaced element.
